TRUSTEES DEMURRER TO PETITION TO DETERMINE VALIDITY OF TRUST PROVISIONS, TO IMPOSE CONSTRUCTIVE TRUST, AND TO COMPEL ACCOUNTING, CALENDAR OF AUG-22-2013 RESPONDENT FRANK WOOS DEMURRER TO ENTIRE PETITION IS HEREBY OVERRULED. RESPONDENT FRANK WOOS DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION TO INVALIDATE THE TRUST AMENDMENTS BASED ON A LACK OF TESTAMENTARY CAPACITY IS HEREBY SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. AMENDED PETITION MUST BE FILED WITH THE COURT WITHIN 10 COURT DAYS FROM THE SCHEDULED HEARING DATE. RESPONDENT FRANK WOOS DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION TO INVALIDATE THE TRUST AMENDMENTS ON THE GROUNDS OF UNDUE INFLUENCE IS HEREBY SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. AMENDED PETITION MUST BE FILED WITH THE COURT WITHIN 10 COURT DAYS FROM THE SCHEDULED HEARING DATE. RESPONDENT FRANK WOOS DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION TO COMPEL AN ACCOUNTING IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. RESPONDENT ALVAREZ CURRENTLY LACKS STANDING TO PETITION TO COMPEL ACCOUNTING AS HE IS NOT A BENEFICIARY TO THE SECOND AMENDED TRUST. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE THE ORDER.

TRUSTEES DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION TO DETERMINE VALIDITY OF TRUST PROVISIONS, TO IMPOSE CONSTRUCTIVE TRUST, AND TO COMPEL ACCOUNTING, CALENDAR OF OCT-16-2013 - THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION TO INVALIDATE THE TRUST AMENDMENTS BASED ON LACK OF TESTAMENTARY CAPACITY, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. PETITIONER HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS. AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION TO INVALIDATE THE TRUST AMENDMENTS BASED ON UNDUE INFLUENCE, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED. PETITIONER HAS REPRESENTED HE WILL DELETE THIS CAUSE OF ACTION IF ALLOWED TO FILE A SECOND AMENDED PETITION. AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION TO COMPEL AN ACCOUNTING, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE PETITIONER IS NOT A BENEFICIARY TO THE SUBJECT TRUST AND CURRENTLY LACKS STANDING TO COMPEL AN ACCOUNTING. FURTHER, PETITIONER HAS REPRESENTED THAT THIS CAUSE OF ACTION WAS INADVERTENTLY INCLUDED IN THE PETITION. AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH EXPECTATION OF INHERITANCE, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED. PETITIONER HAS AN ADEQUATE REMEDY IN PROBATE, THEREFORE HE IS PRECLUDED FROM CLAIMING THIS TORT.

Off Calendar Jun-24-2015 Vacated

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar May-11-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kyong Kim for (successor trustee) Frank Woo.

Ms. Kim reported that the parties have mediated, that she is here on behalf of the parties, that they settled a week after mediation, that they are working out the agreement, that they have notified the Attorney General and are waiting for consent and request that the 6/29/15 trial date be vacated. The status on mediation was taken off calendar. Ms. Kyong was instructed to contact department 206 directly regarding the request to vacate.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. (Not Reported)

Petition To Determine Ownership Of Trust Property (Probate Code Sec. 850)
Off Calendar Mar-02-2015 Continued To Jun-29-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for Ricardo Alvarez. Richard Collier for San Francisco Botancial Garden Society and First Unitarian Church of San Francisco.

The Court noted that Ms. Nelson is not present in court. Mr. Richard reported that the house was put on the market, that an offer was received and approved by his client and that they are ready to mediate and he suggested to return to court at end April. The Court is concerned about how long it took to get to this point and wanted to set a trial date. Counsel's estimate time for trial will be 3 to 5 days. The matter was referred to the Department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial on June 29, 2015 at 9:30 a.m. (order for the same signed in open court and copies were provided to all counsel). Mediation must be completed no later than April 30, 2015. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by May 4, 2015. The Parties are to return on May 11, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Sep-08-2014 Continued To Dec-08-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for Ricardo Alvarez. Kyong Kim for Frank Woo (Successeor Trustee). Richard Collier for S.F. Botanical Society and First Unitarian Church.

Ms. Kim stated that the Petitioner moved out of the residency at the end of June, that the Petitioner is preparing the property for sale and that they have to record a land use covenant (LUC) because there are some contaminates. Ms. Kim also stated that there is a broken pipe, that there is an issue with one of the bank accounts and that the Trustee is working diligently. Ms. Ebey requested 60-days to do discovery. Mr. Collier requested 90-days. The Petition to Determine Ownership of Trust Property and the Petition to Determine Validity of Trust Provisions, etc. were continued to December 8, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mediation is to be completed by that date.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Feb-19-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Milton Katz for and with Ricardo Alvarez.

Judge Mary Wiss recused herself from hearing this matter in the interest of justice. The matter is being heard by Judge Andrew Cheng. Mr. Katz provided Spanish interpretation for Mr. Alvarez. Mr. Katz stated that he and his Client have differences on how to proceed or manage the matter. Mr. Alvarez indicated that he has an appointment with an Attorney this afternoon to see if they will take the case. Mr. Katz requested that future matters be continued. The Court did not grant Mr. Katz' request but noted that it anticipates a request for a continuance from new Counsel. Mr. Katz also informed the Court that two boxes of files were turned over to Mr. Alvarez. The Court granted the Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Nov-25-2013 Continued To Dec-30-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Adult Protective Services To Produce Records
Off Calendar Nov-21-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Milton Katz for Ricardo Alvarez (Moving Party). Sherri Kaiser for Adult Protective Services ("APS"). Kyong Kim for Frank Woo (Successor-Trustee).

During yesterday's hearing, the Court has asked Ms. Van Runkle to go through the records and pick out/select those documents that she feels are directly relevant to Mr. Korf's mental state. Ms. Kaiser presented the Court with the entire record which Ms. Van Randle has tabbed the relevant documents. After reviewing the tabbed portion, the Court determined that the information can be obtained by deposition of the social worker or trial testimony. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the right of privacy versus public interest. At the conclusion of the argument, the Court denied the Motion to Compel Adult Protective Services to produce records with the finding that the information can be obtained by either taking the deposition of the social worker or testimony at trial. Mr. Katz to prepare the order for approval as to form by other counsel.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Adult Protective Services To Produce Records
Off Calendar Nov-20-2013 Continued To Nov-21-13 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Milton Katz for Ricardo Alvarez (Moving Party). Aleeta Van Runkle for Adult Protective Services ("APS"). Kyong Kim for Frank Woo (Successor-Trustee).

The Motion to Compel Adult Protective Services to Produce Records was continued to November 21, 2013 at 2:00 p.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Van Runkle to go through the records and pick out/select those documents that she feels are directly relevant to Mr. Korf's mental state. The Court will then review those documents to determine whether any of them should be released under the Motion for Discovery.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)
